name: Coconut Fried Rice
intro: Next to Jollof rice, fried rice is the most popular rice dish in Nigeria. You’ll find it served at parties and special occasions. It is usually made as a one-pot dish and here we have an elevated version that is cooked in an aromatic coconut broth - irresistible! As you can imagine, fried rice recipes vary from one household to the next, featuring diﬀerent combinations of vegetables and proteins.

ingredients.item: and 1/2 cups tinned full-fat coconut milk
long grain or golden sella basmati rice (washed and drained)
finely chopped roasted mushrooms or tofu
1/2 cup chopped red bell pepper 1/2 cup chopped green bell pepper

method: Heat the coconut oil in a pot and add the chopped onion, garlic and ginger. Fry for 1-2 minutes until the onions start to soften.

Pour in the coconut milk and water, add the rice and season with vegetable stock cubes, curry powder, cayenne pepper and salt. Bring to a boil then stir well, cover the pot and simmer on low heat for 20 minutes.

Add the roasted mushrooms/tofu, red and green bell peppers, green beans, carrots and sweetcorn. Cook for a further 10 minutes, until most of the liquid has been absorbed. Stir well to combine and serve hot with a side of your choice such as Moin Moin (page 156), fried plantain or coleslaw (page 138).
